title: Debugging, Review, and Adversarial Prompting · Eval Recall
summary: Diagnose a failing build and review the repair against a base branch. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an evidence-first debug and review playbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture a minimal reproduction, exact error, environment, and last-known-good boundary before repair.
Rank competing hypotheses and choose discriminating checks.
Prompt for the smallest cause-level repair and a regression test.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reproduction · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Use a changed review case for prompt debug review that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

options.text: The Adversarial Cases record says an evidence-first debug and review playbook looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

This does not satisfy Adversarial Cases for an evidence-first debug and review playbook.
The record names the assumption, applies Adversarial Cases, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test prompts and code against misleading context, malformed inputs, and instruction injection.
